metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_3665
metadata.comment:
Rationale: * Triad3A/RNF216 is an E3 ubiquitin ligase targeting Arc-an activity-regulated gene product that regulates AMPA receptor endocytosis and synaptic plasticity.
*By limiting Arc protein levels induced by synaptic activity, Triad3A regulates Arc-dependent forms of synaptic plasticity.
* Clathrin-Localized Triad3A Controls GluA1 AMPAR Endocytosis (Fig 6)
* Triad3A Is Required for Activity- Induced Upscaling of Synaptic Strength (Fig 7). Experimental description: Supp info: Hippocampal neurons were isolated from E18 RAT embryos and cultured as previously described (Ehlers, 2000).
Homeostatic scaling and baseline mEPSC recordings (Fig 7). Whole-cell voltage clamp recordings were performed on DIV 17-21 hippocampal neurons cultured at high density on poly-D-lysine coated glass coverslips. Species not stated but presumably rat (see above).
DHPG-induced synaptic depression (Fig 8). Hippocampal neuronal cultures were prepared from postnatal day 0 pups from C57BL/6 wild type MICE as described previously (Canal et al., 2011)

checks.label: OWL Consistency
checks.error_message: No inferences attributed to this model graph in the materializer output. Likely a parse failure or other materializer skip — re-run on this model alone to investigate.
